mcqs.question: What is the primary focus of the use case approach?

options.text: Identifying and describing the functional requirements of a system from the user's perspective
Developing a system that meets the needs of the business
Creating a system that is technically sound
Designing a system that is aesthetically pleasing

mcqs.explanation: The use case approach focuses on identifying and describing the functional requirements of a system from the user's perspective.

mcqs.question: What is the role of the actor in a use case?

options.text: The actor is the system being developed
The actor is the user or external system that interacts with the system being developed
The actor is the developer of the system
The actor is the tester of the system

mcqs.explanation: The actor is the user or external system that interacts with the system being developed.

mcqs.question: What is a use case diagram?

options.text: A graphical representation of the system's architecture
A graphical representation of the use cases and actors in a system
A graphical representation of the system's data flow
A graphical representation of the system's control flow

mcqs.explanation: A use case diagram is a graphical representation of the use cases and actors in a system.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a use case scenario?

options.text: To describe the system's architecture
To describe the sequence of events that occurs during a use case
To describe the system's data flow
To describe the system's control flow

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of a use case scenario is to describe the sequence of events that occurs during a use case.

mcqs.question: How do use cases relate to system requirements?

options.text: Use cases are used to identify and define the functional requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the non-functional requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the technical requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the business requirements of a system

mcqs.explanation: Use cases are used to identify and define the functional requirements of a system.

mcqs.question: What is the role of use cases in system testing?

options.text: Use cases are used to develop test cases and scenarios
Use cases are used to develop test plans and scripts
Use cases are used to develop test data and environments
Use cases are used to develop test reports and metrics

mcqs.explanation: Use cases are used to develop test cases and scenarios.

mcqs.question: Can use cases be used for non-functional requirements?

options.text: Yes, use cases can be used to describe non-functional requirements
No, use cases cannot be used to describe non-functional requirements
Maybe, use cases can be used to describe non-functional requirements in some cases
It depends, use cases can be used to describe non-functional requirements depending on the context

mcqs.explanation: Yes, use cases can be used to describe non-functional requirements.

mcqs.question: How do use cases support iterative development?

options.text: Use cases can be refined and updated as the system is developed
Use cases cannot be refined and updated as the system is developed
Use cases can be refined and updated only once during the development process
Use cases cannot be refined and updated at all during the development process

mcqs.explanation: Use cases can be refined and updated as the system is developed.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship between use cases and user stories?

options.text: Use cases and user stories are the same thing
Use cases and user stories are different but related concepts
Use cases are used to describe functional requirements, while user stories are used to describe non-functional requirements
Use cases are used to describe non-functional requirements, while user stories are used to describe functional requirements

mcqs.explanation: Use cases and user stories are different but related concepts.

mcqs.question: What is the primary benefit of using use cases in software development?

options.text: Improved system performance
Increased system security
Enhanced system usability
Better understanding of system requirements

mcqs.explanation: The primary benefit of using use cases is a better understanding of system requirements.

mcqs.question: What is the role of the primary actor in a use case?

options.text: The primary actor is the system being developed
The primary actor is the user or external system that interacts with the system being developed
The primary actor is the developer of the system
The primary actor is the tester of the system

mcqs.explanation: The primary actor is the user or external system that interacts with the system being developed.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a primary actor and a secondary actor?

options.text: A primary actor is the main user of the system, while a secondary actor is a user or system that plays a supporting role
A primary actor is a user or system that plays a supporting role, while a secondary actor is the main user of the system
A primary actor is the developer of the system, while a secondary actor is the tester of the system
A primary actor is the tester of the system, while a secondary actor is the developer of the system

mcqs.explanation: A primary actor is the main user of the system, while a secondary actor is a user or system that plays a supporting role.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a use case scenario?

options.text: To describe the system's architecture
To describe the sequence of events that occurs during a use case
To describe the system's data flow
To describe the system's control flow

mcqs.explanation: The purpose of a use case scenario is to describe the sequence of events that occurs during a use case.

options.text: Use cases are used to identify and define the functional requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the non-functional requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the technical requirements of a system
Use cases are used to identify and define the business requirements of a system

mcqs.explanation: Use cases are used to identify and define the functional requirements of a system.

options.text: Use cases are used to develop test cases and scenarios
Use cases are used to develop test plans and scripts
Use cases are used to develop test data and environments
Use cases are used to develop test reports and metrics

mcqs.explanation: Use cases are used to develop test cases and scenarios.
